signatures.label: print(_: items: Any..., separator: String, terminator: String)

documentation.value: Writes the textual representations of the given items to the standard output.
Zero or more items to print
A string to print between each item (default: `" "`)
A string to print after all items (default: `"\n"`)
Creates a string representing the given value.
A value conforming to `LosslessStringConvertible`
Creates a new integer value from the given string. Returns nil if the string is not a valid integer.
An ASCII representation of a number
Creates a new double-precision floating-point value from the given string.
An ASCII representation of a number
Adds a new element at the end of the array.
The element to append to the array
Returns an array containing the results of mapping the given closure over the sequence's elements.
A mapping closure that accepts an element and returns a transformed value
Returns an array containing only the elements that satisfy the given predicate.
A closure that takes an element and returns a Boolean value indicating whether to include it
Returns a Boolean value indicating whether the sequence contains the given element.
The element to search for in the collection
Creates a new dictionary from the key-value pairs in the given sequence.
A sequence of `(Key, Value)` tuples with unique keys

signatures.label: guard let unwrapped = optional else { return }

documentation.value: Conditionally unwraps an optional. If the optional is nil, the else branch must exit the current scope.
The unwrapped non-optional variable name
The optional value to unwrap
Conditionally unwraps an optional. The body executes only if the optional contains a value.
The unwrapped non-optional variable name
The optional value to unwrap

signatures.label: URLSession.shared.dataTask(with: URL, completionHandler: (Data?, URLResponse?, Error?) -> Void) -> URLSessionDataTask

documentation.value: Creates a task that retrieves the contents of a URL, then calls a handler upon completion.
The URL to retrieve
A closure called with `(data, response, error)` when the task completes

signatures.label: JSONDecoder().decode<T>(_ type: T.Type, from: Data) throws -> T

documentation.value: Decodes a top-level value of the given type from the given JSON representation.
The type of the value to decode (must conform to `Decodable`)
The JSON data to decode
Returns the elements of the sequence, sorted using the given predicate as the comparison.
A predicate that returns `true` if the first argument should be ordered before the second
